cityDNA.coreFantasy: A hobgoblins city where Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement, and Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear turn dry stone paving, civic terraces, dry ground and red brick, wool felt, sandstone, dyed wool, painted canvas into a distinct civic machine.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Varkesh Bastion should feel like an efficient garrison-civilization where gardens, training grounds, intelligence, and hardship all serve preparedness.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Varkesh Bastion organizes public life around order that can feed and defend itself: its districts convert Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement into a readable system of authority, labor, memory, and movement.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Varkesh Bastion should be recognized by Red Brick, Wool Felt, Sandstone, Dyed Wool, Painted Canvas, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Dry Ground, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Mechanical Animus, Yellow Intent Glimmers, Road Warding, and the named anchors Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear, Terrace Granary Beacon, and Gear Inlays Command Bastion.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: The city belongs to hobgoblins; its civic habits should grow from the sidecar-backed district spine rather than generic fantasy urbanism.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear, Terrace Granary Beacon, Gear Inlays Command Bastion, and the five section landmark families symbolize order that can feed and defend itself while warning against command logic hardening into surveillance or class pressure.

canonLocks: Varkesh Bastion is a hobgoblins city, not a generic settlement with cosmetic motifs.
The Wonder is Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear.
The Beacon is Terrace Granary Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Gear Inlays Command Bastion, located at the District 13 authority center.
The five district sections are Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement.
The board should be treated as twenty-five district slots grouped into five section identities.
Section 1 (Agricultural Terraces) preserves these exact anchor names: Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook.
Section 2 (Arena / Training) preserves these exact anchor names: Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, Champion Gate Nook.
Section 3 (Security / Intelligence) preserves these exact anchor names: Security / Intelligence Hall, Cipher Hall, Mist Veils Monument, Dossier Vault Nook.
Section 4 (Underworld / Hidden Economy) preserves these exact anchor names: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook.
Section 5 (Slums / Informal Settlement) preserves these exact anchor names: Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook.
provisionalCanon: Individual district-slot names beyond exact sidecar anchors are organizational labels for the 25-seat board, not new landmark canon.
Minor street customs should be derived from the section spine unless future source files provide more exact anchors.
rejectedReads: Varkesh Bastion as a generic fantasy city without its named Wonder, Beacon, and Seat of Power.
Replacing exact sidecar anchor names with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, wall, or archive.
Treating district section labels as decorative instead of civic systems that drive scenes and assets.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact named anchors from sidecars.
Use the culture identity respectfully as civic design language rather than costume.
Keep all 25 district slots legible even when five slots share one section identity.
Do not add unsupported individual district landmark names beyond the source-backed anchors.

environment.environmentalLogic: Varkesh Bastion's environment should make Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement feel physically necessary. Terrain, light, civic routes, and landmark placement must support movement through all five section identities rather than read as interchangeable scenery.

skyline: Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear is the wonder-scale skyline identifier.
Gear Inlays Command Bastion marks the central authority mass at District 13.
Terrace Granary Beacon must remain visible as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks should form a readable ring of Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement.

civilization.people: Varkesh Bastion's people are hobgoblins; they should be written through shared civic habits, public spaces, work rhythms, and the visible demands of the five section identities.
socialClasses: officials and stewards tied to Gear Inlays Command Bastion
landmark keepers and civic hall administrators
street vendors, porters, runners, and maintenance crews
families living around the section-specific local anchors
civilization.governance: Authority centers on Gear Inlays Command Bastion and is made visible through section civic halls, named landmarks, permissions, public signals, and the citywide response to Terrace Granary Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religious or ceremonial life should be shown through source-backed shrines, temples, wonders, oath spaces, ancestor sites, or ritual geometry when those appear in the district spine.
customs: citizens orient themselves by the section identity of a street before they identify an address
public notices and signals are interpreted through the Beacon and civic halls
named landmarks act as meeting points, proof points, and local memory anchors
section-specific work routines shape food, clothing, posture, and street traffic
District 13 authority matters even when scenes occur in outer district slots
bodyLanguage: citizens check landmarks and signals before crossing into another section
officials use the posture and tools of their section role
workers treat section anchors as practical wayfinding points
crowds become more formal near the Seat of Power and more local near minor landmarks
speechPatterns: directions use exact landmark names rather than generic descriptions
official speech references permits, seals, routes, oaths, or signals as appropriate to the city
working speech is concrete, location-heavy, and tied to civic function
locals distinguish between the Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section landmarks without confusion

streets.appearance: Street scenes should make Varkesh Bastion immediately identifiable through Red Brick, Wool Felt, Sandstone, Dyed Wool, Painted Canvas, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Dry Ground, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Mechanical Animus, Yellow Intent Glimmers, Road Warding, and the exact section anchors rather than generic fantasy facades.
streets.movement: Movement flows through five section identities and twenty-five district slots. People navigate by Terrace Granary Beacon, Gear Inlays Command Bastion, section civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
streets.lighting: Lighting should use sandstone and arcane glow materials, Lantern Lit, Mist Veils, Civic Blue Glow, shared fires, symbolic civic object, with the Beacon and Seat carrying distinct civic emphasis.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should interact with dry stone paving, civic terraces, dry ground and the city's source-backed materials, altering visibility, travel speed, public gathering, and signal readability.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime emphasizes section labor: Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement. Civic halls, shops, and public works create the main public rhythm.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes should preserve the city's identity through landmark silhouettes, beacon light, watch routes, late shops, ritual spaces, and section-specific sounds.

prestigious: workshops or offices near Gear Inlays Command Bastion
specialist houses serving Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear
master artisans tied to section landmarks

housing.wealthy: High-status housing clusters near Gear Inlays Command Bastion, major terraces, and prestigious landmark approaches; it should display the city materials cleanly and control sightlines toward Wonder or Beacon.
housing.middle: Middle housing occupies upper rooms, side courts, work-family compounds, and mixed-use streets close to civic halls and section services.
housing.poor: Poorer housing should be practical and specific to local labor, with patched materials, shared courtyards, crowded lanes, and strong dependence on nearby section anchors.

wonder.name: Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of order that can feed and defend itself made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A wonder-scale skyline anchor using Red Brick, Wool Felt, Sandstone, Dyed Wool, Painted Canvas, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Dry Ground, and the hobgoblins visual language.
wonder.civicRole: Represents the whole city above the five sections: Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement.
storyHooks: access to Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear changes public legitimacy
a citywide ritual reveals hidden pressure
the Wonder becomes a navigation or warning reference

beacon.name: Terrace Granary Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal and shared alarm for Varkesh Bastion.
beacon.visualRole: A section-scale signal landmark that must read immediately as the city Beacon.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates public response, civic warnings, and Conjunction-state attention.
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a signal
the Beacon redirects traffic between sections
a warning exposes which citizens trust civic authority

seatOfPower.civicRole: Seat of government, petitions, records, rulings, and command visibility.

a sealed record changes public interpretation

districtDioramaGuidance: Create twenty-five district dioramas grouped into five recognizable sections: Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement.
Each district should use its districtNumber and sectionNumber, but avoid baked text labels in imagery.
D13 must read as the Seat of Power district centered on Gear Inlays Command Bastion.
Repeated section anchors may appear across five related slots, but each slot needs its own street function, crowd density, and prop emphasis.
Use exact source-backed anchor names for file naming, prompt references, and canon cross-checking.
S1 must preserve Agricultural Terraces with anchors: Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook.
S2 must preserve Arena / Training with anchors: Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, Champion Gate Nook.
S3 must preserve Security / Intelligence with anchors: Security / Intelligence Hall, Cipher Hall, Mist Veils Monument, Dossier Vault Nook.
S4 must preserve Underworld / Hidden Economy with anchors: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook.
S5 must preserve Slums / Informal Settlement with anchors: Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds should keep Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear as the skyline symbol, Gear Inlays Command Bastion at the central civic authority, and Terrace Granary Beacon as the readable public signal.

Arrange the five section identities so Varkesh Bastion does not converge with other cities: Agricultural Terraces, Arena / Training, Security / Intelligence, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Slums / Informal Settlement.

Use terrain cues (Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Dry Ground), material cues (Red Brick, Wool Felt, Sandstone, Dyed Wool, Painted Canvas), and magic cues (Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Mechanical Animus, Yellow Intent Glimmers, Road Warding) consistently across district clusters.
Show roads, terraces, gates, courts, or paths that make the twenty-five-district tactical layout plausible.
requiredVisualElements: Hobgoblin Banner-Fortress of the Ordered Spear
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or embedded generation notes
generic fantasy city assets that ignore exact anchor names
unlabeled invented landmarks replacing sidecar names
flattening five sections into one visual district
treating culture identity as costume rather than civic design
overcrowded detail that hides Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power
